meta_title: For Students: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Debugging: Content Creation for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to leverage content creation and AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development workflow today.
content.answer_block: Transform debugging from a roadblock into a growth engine. By documenting your troubleshooting process as content, you externalize your logic, forcing clarity. Use Antigravity to architect robust systems and Claude Code to identify root causes, turning every error message into a high-value technical artifact for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You are staring at a terminal full of red text, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat in a void. You have been stuck for hours, and the Imposter Syndrome is beginning to settle in. Every senior dev has been exactly where you are—trapped in a logic loop, unable to parse why your stack trace is failing. But here is the secret: the code isn't the problem; it's your process.

Great engineers don't just fix bugs; they narrate their recovery. By integrating content creation into your debugging loop, you shift from a passive victim of errors to an active architect of solutions. This isn't just about fixing the app; it's about building the narrative of a world-class builder. Let’s turn that frustration into your next portfolio piece.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Logic
steps.description: Before you fix it, document the failure state. Create a concise 'bug report' entry that explains the expected vs. actual output.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following code block and generate a root cause hypothesis based on the current stack trace and variable state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file-path ./src/main.py --instruction 'Find the memory leak in this function'

steps.title: Architect the Fix with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map out the dependency graph, ensuring your proposed fix doesn't trigger a cascading failure elsewhere.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map out the dependencies for this module and suggest a refactor that isolates the error handling logic.

steps.title: Execute Refactor via Claude Code
steps.description: Push the fix to a new branch and use Claude Code to perform a dry-run test of the changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a unit test that specifically targets the edge case that caused the crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'fix: resolve race condition in state management'

steps.title: Synthesize the 'How-To' Content
steps.description: Convert your technical notes into a short, punchy 'TIL' (Today I Learned) post for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the debugging process I just performed into a professional LinkedIn-style technical case study.
steps.claude_code_command: claude chat 'Draft a blog post explaining this bug and the architectural fix we just implemented'

steps.title: Automate Future Regression Checks
steps.description: Use your new code as a template for future projects, ensuring you never repeat the same mistake.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate template based on this corrected logic for future use in my AI projects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate a regression test suite for this module'

pro_tips: Treat every cryptic error message as a prompt to explain the system architecture to a peer.
Never debug in silence; use your IDE's terminal to record the thought process behind every line change.
Use Claude Code to perform 'Rubber Ducking' by explaining your code logic back to the terminal.
Build a 'Common Failure' library in your Notion; categorize errors by the framework used.
When stuck, force yourself to write a 100-word explanation of the bug; the solution often appears while writing.
resources: Effective Technical Writing for Devs: https://dev.to/guide
content.next_steps: Now that you have fixed your bug and documented the process, deploy your updated app to a live environment and link your new technical post to your GitHub README.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the mindset to stop debugging and start building. Don't let the next error stop your momentum—join the network of builders who turn every bug into a breakthrough. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
